sam.name: Solid Earth

models.name: Underworld2 Training
models.infoMessage: Underworld 2 is a python-friendly version of the Underworld code which provides a programmable and flexible front end to all the functionality of the code running in a parallel HPC environment.

models.name: GPlates Portal
models.infoMessage: The GPlates Portal is a web-based platform that provides interactive 3D visualizations of models over geological time, such as plate tectonics and dynamic topography. Other capabilities include the display of global geoscience datasets such as seafloor lithology and gravity anomalies. It allows the user to explore the models and datasets without the need to install software, all within a web browser.

models.name: GPlates
models.infoMessage: GPlates is a plate tectonics program. Manipulate reconstructions of geological and paleogeographic features through geological time. Interactively visualize vector, raster and volume data. PyGPlates is the GPlates Python library. Get fine-grained access to GPlates functionality in your Python scripts.

sam.infoMessage: Applications from AuScope's Simulation, Analysis & Modelling program (SAM)

portal.name: AuScope Portal

models.name: AuScope Portal
models.infoMessage: A web-based interface for searching and accessing a vast treasure trove of data, information, imagery, services and applications.

portal.infoMessage: AVRE's web-based interface for searching and accessing a vast treasure trove of data.

models.name: EarthBank
models.infoMessage: EarthBank provides the geochemistry community with a bespoke tool to explore, upload, disseminate and extract geo-sample and geochemistry data in a global geospatial context

models.name: AusPass
models.infoMessage: AusPass is a service dedicated to the acquisition, management, and distribution of passive seismological data in Australia.

models.name: SENSI
models.infoMessage: Seismic Network Design App for optimising seismic array design that is based on the analytical method called SENSI that has been developed by Tramelli et al. (2013), which is widely used around the world including the GipNet array deployed to monitor seismicity in the Gippsland region in Victoria, Australia.

models.name: AuSIS Map
models.infoMessage: The Australian Seismometers in Schools program provides both education outreach and quality data for monitoring and research. Explore the affect of earthquakes on AuScope's network of school-based seismometers. Click on a school to see plots of seismic waveforms. Explore worldwide earthquake events and their distances and travel time to Australia. Earthquake event data courtesy of Geoscience Australia.

3dmodels.name: 3d Geoscience Models

models.name: AuScope 3d Geoscience Model Portal
models.infoMessage: This website enables you to explore a wide range of geoscience models from all around Australia in 3D. The models are supported by links to other informative sources, namely AuScope Portal, Geoscience Australia, and the state and territory geoscience surveys' websites.

3dmodels.infoMessage: AVRE's application for viewing geoscience models in 3D

datarepo.name: Data Repository

models.name: AuScope Data Repository
models.infoMessage: The AuScope Data Repository is dedicated to the preservation and continuous accessibility of geoscientific data generated by Australia's geoscience community, addressing fundamental geoscience inquiries and overarching challenges such as climate change, natural resource security, and natural hazards. Its features include: AAF login, automated DOI (Digital Object Identifier) minting, a curated publication process (with optional embargo period), and integration with ARDC Research Data Australia.

datarepo.infoMessage: The AuScope Data Repository is a trusted repository for depositing geoscience data.

geophys.name: Geophysics

models.name: Geophysics HPC
models.infoMessage: The Geophysics community space contains documentation and tutorials on how to access geophysical datasets at NCI as well as the various processing and inversion tools available on NCI’s Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) and Gadi Supercomputer.

geophys.infoMessage: Geophysics applications from AuScope AVRE, NCI and the geophysics community

iEarth.name: AuScope Inversion Laboratory

models.name: iEarth Software
models.infoMessage: The AuScope inversion laboratory (2011-2014) (ilab) is a project to make advanced inference and data analysis software available to the geophysics community. Many of the codes have been developed specifically for iLab, with others contributed by the authors. Software provided here has been possible with development support from the AuScope-AGOS (2011-2014). The AuScope Inversion Laboratory is a component of the Australian Geophysical Observing system (AuScope-AGOS) funded through the Australian Federal Government’s Education Infrastructure Fund (EIF3).

iEarth.infoMessage: iEarth is a collaborative group comprising scientists from academia, government, and industry

mate.name: Model Atlas of the Earth

models.name: Model Atlas of the Earth
models.infoMessage: M@TE is an open-source collection of computational models with a focus on tectonics, hydrogeology and surface processes. M@TE provides a range of digital infrastructure to support the FAIR principles: Findability, Accessibility, Interoperability and Reusability.

mate.infoMessage: M@TE is an open-source collection of computational models with a focus on tectonics, hydrogeology and surface processes.

NVCL.name: National Virtual Core Library (NVCL)

models.name: NVCL Map
models.infoMessage: The National Virtual Core Library (NVCL) is a web-based platform that provides access to a wide range of geological core samples and related data from across Australia. It allows users to explore and analyze core samples, view high-resolution images, and access associated metadata.

NVCL.infoMessage: Explore the rollout of the world's largest online drill core mineralogical database, freely accessible to users anywhere in the world.
